steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: After claiming the statues are extremely heavy, the passage provides concrete proof. The transition must signal that supporting evidence is coming.

steps.title: Match the signal
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is confirmation/emphasis. The first sentence claims the statues are "extremely heavy." The second sentence provides a concrete number (more than 5 tons each) that proves that claim. The blank needs a transition signaling supporting evidence.
  • Choice A — In fact,: signals that the second sentence confirms and reinforces the preceding claim with concrete evidence, fitting perfectly. ✓
  • Choice B — Earlier,: signals a time shift to a prior point. No time contrast is being drawn — the weight figure is not from an earlier period. ✗
  • Choice C — In any case,: signals a concession or "regardless" pivot — acknowledging another viewpoint before moving on. No alternative viewpoint is present. ✗
  • Choice D — Instead,: signals that the second sentence replaces or contradicts the first. Giving the actual weight confirms the claim of being heavy; it does not replace or contradict it. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
